title: Diameter of Binary Tree
problem_statement: Return the length of the longest path between any two nodes in a binary tree.

examples.explanation: The diameter path goes through nodes [4,2,1,3] or [5,2,1,3].

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 10^4]
intuition: Use recursion to compute height and update diameter as the sum of left and right heights.

approaches.name: Post-order height computation
approaches.description: Compute subtree heights and update the max diameter at each node as left height plus right height.
approaches.why_not: Computing all pairwise node distances is O(n^2) and unnecessary.

interview_qa.q: What is the diameter definition here?
interview_qa.a: The number of edges on the longest path between two nodes.
It computes subtree depths while also capturing the longest path through each node.
